vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-1132
vulnerability.description: Keycloak redirect_uri validation bypass via path traversal — authorization code interception

statements.impact_statement: FedRAMP HIGH CRITICAL — IA-8 VIOLATION: Keycloak 23.0.3 is the OIDC identity provider for all government user authentication including CAC/PIV smart card login. The redirect_uri bypass allows an attacker to steal the OAuth2 authorization code by crafting a phishing link that appears to redirect to a legitimate government domain but actually redirects to an attacker-controlled endpoint after Keycloak validation. Government users with CUI access can have their authorization codes stolen without any indication of compromise. This is an active authentication bypass against a FedRAMP High system.
statements.action_statement: IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED: Upgrade Keycloak to 24.0.3. This is a FedRAMP ATO-impacting vulnerability. Notify the Authorizing Official (AO) as a significant change per FedRAMP requirements. Until patched: restrict redirect_uri registrations to exact matches only (no wildcard or pattern matching). Audit Keycloak realm configuration to remove all wildcard redirect URIs. Implement WAF rules to detect and block redirect_uri path traversal sequences (%2F, %2f, %252F).

vulnerability.name: CVE-2023-5077
vulnerability.description: HashiCorp Vault PKI issuer configuration overwrite — persistent CA root replacement

statements.impact_statement: Vault 1.15.4 includes the fix for CVE-2023-5077 (fixed in 1.15.2). The SBOM version 1.15.4 is a patched version. The CVE affects Vault 1.12.0 through 1.15.1. The GovCloud platform deployed version 1.15.4, which was selected specifically to include this security patch. Additionally, the Vault PKI issuer write endpoint is restricted to a dedicated PKI-admin service account with a separate Vault token, not accessible to application roles.
statements.action_statement: No action required for CVE-2023-5077 — current version 1.15.4 is patched. Continue monitoring for new Vault CVEs. Next planned Vault upgrade: 1.16.x in Q2 2026 per change management schedule.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-23322
vulnerability.description: Envoy proxy rate-limiter race condition causing worker thread crash on stream reset

statements.impact_statement: FedRAMP SC-8 IMPACT: Istio 1.20.1 uses Envoy proxy 1.28.x as the sidecar, which is vulnerable to this DoS. An attacker who can send HTTP requests to any Istio-meshed service (including through the Kubernetes API) can crash Envoy worker threads, disrupting mTLS between microservices. This would cause service-to-service communication failures and could break CUI document processing workflows. NIST 800-53 SC-8 requires protection of transmitted information — mTLS disruption violates this control.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade Istio to 1.20.3+ which includes Envoy with the race condition fix. Before upgrading, test in the GovCloud staging environment. Coordinate upgrade with the Authorizing Official as an Istio version change may require SSP documentation update.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2023-44487
vulnerability.description: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset DDoS (CVSS 7.5) — CISA KEV listed

statements.impact_statement: ASP.NET Core 8.0 RTM (8.0.0) includes the Kestrel patch for HTTP/2 Rapid Reset published in November 2023 cumulative update (8.0.0-rtm.23519.4). The GovCloud platform uses .NET 8.0 with the November 2023 cumulative patches applied. AWS GovCloud ALB and CloudFront (if used) have also been patched by AWS at the infrastructure layer. This CVE is CISA KEV listed — confirmed patched status is essential for FedRAMP continuous monitoring.
statements.action_statement: No action required — ASP.NET Core 8.0 RTM includes the patch. Document in SSP continuous monitoring log that CVE-2023-44487 was assessed and confirmed remediated as of the November 2023 patch cycle. Verify runtime version in production: dotnet --version should show 8.0.x with November 2023+ build date.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-21626
vulnerability.description: runc Leaky Vessels container escape via /proc/self/fd file descriptor leak

statements.impact_statement: CISA KEV LISTED — FedRAMP SI-2 requires patching of KEV entries within defined timelines. Investigating whether the EKS GovCloud node group is running runc 1.1.12+ or an older version. EKS 1.28.x typically ships with containerd 1.7.x which may include runc 1.1.10 or 1.1.11 — both vulnerable. Container escape would allow an attacker with pod exec access to compromise the underlying EC2 instance, accessing other government workload pods. CISA binding operational directive requires patching ASAP.
statements.action_statement: URGENT: Verify runc version on EKS nodes via 'kubectl debug node -it --image=amazonlinux -- runc --version'. If runc < 1.1.12, update EKS managed node groups to latest AMI which includes runc 1.1.12. Force node rotation to replace all existing instances. This is a CISA KEV — FedRAMP requires remediation within 2 weeks of KEV listing. Report status to Authorizing Official immediately.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-28182
vulnerability.description: nghttp2 HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame unbounded memory consumption

statements.impact_statement: FedRAMP SI-2: .NET 8 Kestrel uses nghttp2 for HTTP/2 support. Versions before 8.0.4 (April 2024 patch) are vulnerable to the HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame flood. The GovCloud platform is running .NET 8.0.0 and has not applied the April 2024 cumulative update. An attacker can exhaust memory on the ASP.NET Core server processing CUI data, causing an OOM crash. As a government system, the platform is a high-value DoS target.
statements.action_statement: Apply .NET 8.0.4 cumulative update (April 2024). Plan maintenance window for GovCloud production. The update includes the nghttp2 1.61.0 patch embedded in the Kestrel runtime. After update, verify dotnet runtime version shows 8.0.4+. Document in SSP change log.
